questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: When photons of energy 4.25 eV strike the surface of a metal A , the ejected photoelectrons have maximum kinetic energy, $\mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{A}}$ expressed in eV and de Broglie wavelength $\lambda_{\mathrm{A}}$. The maximum kinetic energy of photoelectrons liberated from another metal B by photons of energy 4.70 eV is $T_B=\left(T_A-1.50 \mathrm{eV}\right)$. If the de Broglie wavelength of these photoelectrons is $\lambda_B=2 \lambda_A$, then :
options: the work function of A is 2.25 eV
the work function of B is 4.20 eV
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & h v=\text { K.E. }(T)+\text { work function }(\mathrm{W}) \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{hv}=\mathrm{T}+\mathrm{W} \\ & \Rightarrow 4.25 \mathrm{eV}=\mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{A}}+\mathrm{W}_{\mathrm{A}}(\text { for Metal } A) \\ & \Rightarrow 4.70 \mathrm{eV}=\mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{B}}+\mathrm{W}_{\mathrm{B}}(\text { for Metal } B)\end{aligned}$
Since $\quad T_B=\left(T_A-1.5\right) \mathrm{eV}$
Also $\quad \lambda=\mathrm{h} / \mathrm{p}$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \lambda=\frac{\mathrm{h}}{\sqrt{2 \mathrm{mT}}}\left\{\because \frac{\mathrm{p}^2}{2 \mathrm{~m}}=\mathrm{T}=\text { K.E. }\right\} \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{\lambda_{\mathrm{A}}}{\lambda_{\mathrm{B}}}=\sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{B}}}{\mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{A}}}}\end{aligned}$
Since $\lambda_A=\frac{1}{2} \lambda_B$
$\begin{aligned} \Rightarrow & T_A=4 T_B \\ \Rightarrow & T_B=T_A-1.50 \text { gives } \\ & T_B=4 T_B-1.5 \\ \Rightarrow & T_B=0.5 \mathrm{eV} \\ \Rightarrow T_A & =2 \mathrm{eV} \\ \Rightarrow W_A & =2.25 \mathrm{eV} \\ \Rightarrow & W_B=4.20 \mathrm{EV}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: $\text { Photoelectric effect supports the quantum nature of light because: }$
options: $\text { There is a minimum frequency of light below which no photoelectrons are emitted. }$
The maximum K.E. of photoelectrons depends only on the frequency of light and not on its intensity.
Even when the metal surface is faintly illuminated by light of wavelength less than the threshold wavelength, the photoelectrons leave the surface immediately.
$\text { Electric charge of photoelectrons is quantized. }$
questions.solution: $\text { Existence of cut-off frequency and photoemission takes place even when intensity is low. }$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Consider white light whose wavelength spread is from 400 nm to 700 nm . Its energy is uniformly distributed in this spectrum (i.e. $\frac{d E}{d \lambda}=$ constant, where $d E$ is the energy of the photons wavelengths between $\lambda$ and $\lambda+d \lambda$ ). The light is incident on a metal $A$ of work function 1.55 eV . Saturation photo current is 6 mA . Now the same light is incident on metal $B$ of work function 2.48 eV . Choose the correct options [Tale hc = 1240 eV nm . Assume photo efficiency remains same]
options: Stopping potential for experiment with metal $A$ is 0.22 V
Stopping potential for experiment with metal $B$ is 0.62 V
Saturation photo current for metal $B$ will be 2 mA
Saturation photo current for metal $B$ will be $\frac{18}{11} \mathrm{~mA}$
questions.solution:
Let $d n$ be number of photons from $\lambda$ to $\lambda+d \lambda$
$\begin{aligned}
& d E=d n \frac{(h c)}{\lambda} \\
& \frac{d E}{d \lambda}=k \\
& \int_0^n(d n) h c=k \int_{400}^{\lambda_{t n}} \lambda d \lambda
\end{aligned}$

For $A: \lambda_{t n}=800 \mathrm{~nm}$
For $B: \lambda_{t n}=500 \mathrm{~nm}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \frac{I_1}{I_2}=\frac{6}{I_2}=\frac{7^2-4^2}{5^2-4^2} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad I_2=\frac{18}{11} m A
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
